title: eugenia rigidifolia subsp. rangelensis
intro: Eugenia rigidifolia subsp. rangelensis, also known as Rangel's Eugenia, is a plant species endemic to Mexico and is a subspecies of Eugenia rigidifolia. The plant has been reported to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential medicinal value. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and inflammation, due to its health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. Eugenia rigidifolia subsp. rangelensis can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in herbal remedies, highlighting its significance in traditional medicine.
